steps.title: Plug in the lower bound of the training intensity
steps.content:
The recommended range is $0.5 \le p \le 0.85$. Substitute $p = 0.5$:
$$H = 120(0.5) + 60 = 60 + 60 = 120 \text{ bpm}$$

steps.title: Plug in the upper bound of the training intensity
steps.content:
Substitute $p = 0.85$:
$$H = 120(0.85) + 60 = 102 + 60 = 162 \text{ bpm}$$
Since the formula is linear with a positive slope, $H$ increases as $p$ increases, so the training heart rate range is $120 \le H \le 162$.

steps.title: Evaluate each answer choice
steps.content:
• A: $120 \le H \le 162$ — The lower bound is 120 and the upper bound is 162, which matches our calculations. That matches choice A — A is correct.
  • B: Upper bound 120 — This would mean the maximum is 120, but 120 is the minimum training heart rate. Incorrect.
  • C: Upper bound 162 with a different lower bound — The lower bound shown is not 120, so this does not accurately represent the range. Incorrect.
  • D: Upper bound 102 — $H = 102$ corresponds to $p = 0.35$, which is below the recommended range. Incorrect.
